headings.usage: Used sparingly for panel titles, empty state headings, and modal headers. Keep letter spacing tight (tracking-tight).

body.usage: Primary UI font. Used for toolbox labels, button text, properties panel inputs. Clean, technical, and readable.

code_and_data.usage: Used for Monaco editor font, live XML preview, dimension inputs (e.g., '210mm'), and exact coordinates on the canvas.

main_structure.description: Full height (h-screen) with overflow hidden. Fixed sidebars, fluid center.

panels.header_toolbar: h-12 border-b border-zinc-800 bg-zinc-950 flex items-center justify-between px-4
panels.left_toolbox: w-[280px] border-r border-zinc-800 bg-zinc-950 flex flex-col h-[calc(100vh-3rem)] overflow-y-auto
panels.center_canvas: flex-1 flex flex-col h-[calc(100vh-3rem)] bg-black relative overflow-auto items-center justify-center bg-[radial-gradient(#27272A_1px,transparent_1px)] [background-size:20px_20px]
panels.right_preview: w-[400px] border-l border-zinc-800 bg-[#1E1E1E] flex flex-col h-[calc(100vh-3rem)]

layouts.surfaces: Grid Borders strategy. The UI skeleton should be exposed. Use 1px subtle borders (border-zinc-800) between all list items, tool sections, and panels. No soft drop-shadows on the UI panels, just sharp technical lines.

canvas.description: The WYSIWYG area. The 'paper' element must have a stark white background (unless overridden) and sharp drop-shadow to contrast against the dark workspace.
canvas.interaction: Elements on canvas should have a 1px dashed cyan border on hover, and a solid 2px cyan border with resize handles (small white squares with cyan border) on select.

monaco_editor.customization: Bind to XSL-FO XML generation state. Background matches #1E1E1E.

toolbox_buttons.style: Flat, compact. No card-like shadows. Hover state triggers a bg-zinc-800/50 fill and white text.

properties_panel.inputs: Shadcn UI inputs customized to be compact (h-8). Dark bg (bg-zinc-900), no outer ring unless focused. Font sizes should be text-xs or text-sm for a dense, Pro-tool feel.

visual_enhancers.micro_interactions: Fast hover states (transition-colors duration-150). Tooltips for all toolbar actions (Undo, Redo, Zoom).
visual_enhancers.empty_states: Rich empty states in the left properties panel if no element is selected. Use an outline icon (Lucide) + muted text.
visual_enhancers.custom_cursors: Use `cursor-crosshair` when an add-tool (Text/Image) is active, `cursor-move` on hover of draggable elements, `cursor-ew-resize` / `cursor-ns-resize` on handles.

motion.philosophy: Utilitarian and rapid. Developer tools should not have slow bouncy animations.

accessibility.contrast: Ensure text-to-background contrast ratio > 4.5:1. Primary text must be pure white on the zinc-950 background.
accessibility.focus: All interactive elements must have a ring-2 ring-sky-400 focus state.
accessibility.testing: Add data-testid attributes to all major interactive elements (e.g., data-testid='toolbox-add-text', data-testid='canvas-element', data-testid='xml-preview-copy').

media.placeholder_images: Support base64 or placeholder URLs for testing XSL-FO generation. Allow background image upload via File API.

3d_libraries.usage: Not applicable for this 2D professional editor. Stick to 2D transforms and DOM manipulation.

image_urls.description: Ornate gold border on cream background, perfect to use as a placeholder certificate background inside the WYSIWYG canvas for testing.

UNIVERSAL GUIDELINES FOR MAIN AGENT: You MUST build this as a frontend-only React + Tailwind + Shadcn application.
NO generic centered layouts. The tool must look like a professional dense IDE (like VSCode, Figma, or Framer).
Use Lucide React for sharp, technical icons.
Implement the 3-panel split correctly: fixed left toolbox/properties, fluid center with zoom/panning canvas, fixed right monaco preview.
Ensure Monaco Editor is loaded and bound to the state of the canvas to display live XSL-FO output.
Do NOT use pure transparent backgrounds on panels; use solid zinc-900 or zinc-950.
Use generic HTML5 Drag and Drop or a robust library like `react-rnd` (Draggable and Resizable) for the canvas elements.
Canvas element state (x, y, width, height, text/image content, formatting) must be managed centrally to power both the Canvas rendering and the Monaco XML generation.
Provide a dark theme UI but the WYSIWYG paper itself is light/white unless a dark background is uploaded.
Toasts must use Sonner for notifications (e.g., 'XML Copied to Clipboard', 'Template Saved').
Ensure the XSL-FO code generated matches the XSL-FO 1.0 standard structurally (<fo:root>, <fo:layout-master-set>, <fo:page-sequence>).

instructions_to_main_agent.step_1: Setup the layout skeleton (Header/Toolbar at top, Left Sidebar for tools/properties, Center relative Canvas area, Right Sidebar for Monaco editor).
instructions_to_main_agent.step_2: Implement central state using Zustand or React Context to hold elements (text blocks, images) with their properties (x, y, w, h, styles, base64 data).
instructions_to_main_agent.step_3: Build the WYSIWYG canvas using react-rnd or custom drag logic, allowing elements to be positioned freely. Apply zoom via CSS transform scale() on the canvas container.
instructions_to_main_agent.step_4: Build properties panel context-awareness (clicking a text element updates the left panel to show font size, weight, color controls).
instructions_to_main_agent.step_5: Implement the XSL-FO generator function that takes the current state and formats it into proper XSL-FO 1.0 XML syntax. Bind this to Monaco as a read-only live preview.
instructions_to_main_agent.step_6: Wire up Undo/Redo (state snapshots), grid toggle, and LocalStorage persistence logic.
